post.content:
Telephonic Round:
[Word Abbreviation.](https://leetcode.com/problems/word-abbreviation)(Premium)

Onsite:

Round 1: (Problem Solving)
A similar problem to [01 Matrix](https://leetcode.com/problems/01-matrix/)
[Number of islands 2](https://leetcode.com/problems/number-of-islands-ii/)(Premium)

Round 2:(Data Structure)
[Design Tic Tac Toe](https://leetcode.com/problems/design-tic-tac-toe/)(Premium)
[Side length of a square](https://leetcode.com/problems/maximum-side-length-of-a-square-with-sum-less-than-or-equal-to-threshold/description/)

Round 3:(Architecture/System Design)
Design distributed notification service.

Round 4:(Architecture/System Design)
Design a proximity service like Yelp.

Round 5:(Hiring manager)
Simple behavioral questions like *Why do u want to join Uber. ?*

Round 6:(Bar raiser)
Culture Fit.
Previous Experience & Current project in Depth.
Design a meeting scheduler.
A tree problem similar to [Distribute coins](https://leetcode.com/problems/distribute-coins-in-binary-tree)

Uber expects production level code for every PS/DS problem. The interviewer did dry run with multiple test cases.
Would really want to thank the Leetcode community for all the help.

Such an informative post! It would be really helpful if you could answer these 2 questions. 
1. Did you apply through a referral? 
2. Do personal projects/side projects play a role?

Thanks!!!

Posting again here for everyone to see.

OA[60 min]:
1. One two pointer problem. Given two integer arrays A and B, find the count of elements in A which are larger by k than every element in B. `int getCount(int[] A, int[] B, int k)`
2. k window maximum.

Data structure [on zoom call, 60 min]:
1. Given array of roads in a graph which are of 3 types: 1 can be walked by men, one can be walked by women and one can be walked by both, find out maximum number of roads that can be removed from the city such that both men and women can access all locations.
2. Given 10^9 edges on a graph, stored in a file or coming as a stream, formulate a way to find minimum cost to build Minimum Spanning Tree.

Machine coding[45 min]:
1. Code a general purpose scheduler library that can be used in a project, where we can schedule 3 types of task, wanted multithreaded implementation where we can provide a concurrency limit.

Design round [60 min]:
Design heatmap service for Uber.

Lunch.
HM round: HM denied the interview.
I was done for the day and later received a polite no email from the HR.

There wasn't any machine coding round? I was rejected only because of that, I solved both questions in Online Assessment test and then solved questions in one data structures and algorithm round. Got soft yes on System design and architecture round as well.

Feeling really sad because that team really is on hiring mode and according to my experience bar wasn't that high as compared to your interview experience.
I was asked to design a general purpose scheduler, multithreaded implementation in the machine coding round.
These look really easy. Tic tac toe is like generate the heuristics manually and done.
Commenting it here to get noticed if possible please suggest,\n\nhttps://leetcode.com/discuss/career/747288/Suggestions-for-Internship-Interview-Preperation-in-6-months-and-SDE-1-in-a-year
> Given 10^9 edges on a graph, stored in a file or coming as a stream, formulate a way to find minimum cost to build Minimum Spanning Tree.\n\nCan someone share hints or a solution to this problem?
Do they expect us to write the whole code in any of the above rounds or just pseudo code is fine ?\n

The word abbreviation question is too hard I will say for a telephonic round :( And surprised that both questions in first round are BFS based. Overall, above average difficulty level questions. Looks like uber process is quite hard.
Which resources did you use for System Design rounds? [educative.io?]\nAnd could you please give a brief about the problem "Design distributed notification service." [resource or approach anything]\n
What was the duration of the bar raiser round? 4  questions seem a lot. Are you supposed to write code for all your answers in the bar raiser round?
Round 3 was a machine coding round ?
What is your experience? I also want to apply for Uber but I don\'t see any open positions for freshers. Is there any chance I can still apply through a referral?
